name: Coast Guard Sector Jacksonville
tagline: The Coast Guard guarding northeast Florida's coast and the busy Port of Jacksonville.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Jacksonville (Jacksonville, FL; Atlantic Beach area). CONUS full template (BAH MHA FL058 Jacksonville; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Port security for the Port of Jacksonville + SAR/law enforcement on the NE FL coast. Part of the large Jacksonville Navy/CG complex (NAS Jax + Mayport already in app). Medical: CG clinic + access to Naval Hospital Jacksonville + TRICARE network. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Florida is TRICARE East (no state income tax). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 32233 (Atlantic Beach).

data.Role: Coast Guard hub for northeast Florida
data.Mission: Port security for the Port of Jacksonville, search and rescue, and law enforcement
data.Note: Florida has no state income tax

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Jacksonville guards the northeast Florida coast and the busy Port of Jacksonville, running port security, search and rescue, and law enforcement.

It's in the Jacksonville metro, part of a large Navy and Coast Guard community (alongside NAS Jacksonville and Naval Station Mayport), with an affordable-for-the-coast cost of living and no state income tax.

sections.title: Beach or Inland: Affordable Jacksonville Living

data.subtitle: Jacksonville is affordable for a coastal metro, with beaches and a big military community. The main choice is how close to the water you want to be versus commute and schools.
intro: The Jacksonville area is one of the more affordable coastal metros, with no state income tax, so BAH stretches. Coast Guard housing is limited; many also use Navy housing across the Jacksonville complex.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across the beaches (Atlantic/Neptune/Jax Beach), the city, and St. Johns County to the south.

recommendations.advice: Atlantic Beach, Neptune Beach, and Jacksonville Beach put you by the Atlantic.
St. Johns County (well regarded for schools) and inland Jacksonville offer more space for the money.
The area is full of Navy/CG families; housing across the Jacksonville complex is an option.
It's the Atlantic coast; budget for coastal insurance and know your evacuation plan.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing is limited, but the large Jacksonville Navy complex offers military housing options too; many families also live off base.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA FL058 (Jacksonville) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Duval County serves Jacksonville and the beaches, and St. Johns County to the south is highly regarded.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; family care via the TRICARE network and access to Naval Hospital Jacksonville

data.Exchange: Coast Guard Exchange (CGX) / access to Navy exchanges in the area
data.Commissary: Access to Jacksonville-area DoD commissaries (NAS Jacksonville, NS Mayport)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and Jacksonville's affordability and no income tax make buying a real option, with many sailors/Coast Guardsmen returning to the area.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your rotation feels uncertain.
You want time to learn the metro and pick beachside versus inland.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing or coastal insurance.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer tour or repeat Jacksonville-area assignments.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable Jacksonville market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but Jacksonville's affordability and no income tax make buying worth a look for longer tours. Factor in coastal insurance, run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on the beaches, the city, and St. Johns County (and Navy housing options) before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Florida vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Duval or St. Johns County) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment; the CG clinic plus Naval Hospital Jacksonville and the network serve families.

data.a: It's the Coast Guard's hub for northeast Florida, guarding the Port of Jacksonville and the coast with port security, search and rescue, and law enforcement.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. Jacksonville is MHA FL058.
Across the metro: the beaches (Atlantic/Neptune/Jax Beach), the city, and St. Johns County (great schools). It's a big Navy/CG community, so military housing is an option too.
Yes, for a coastal metro, helped by no state income tax. Factor in coastal insurance if you buy.
